div.div: Implementation notes (trimmed from boxes above — verified against code 2026-06-30)

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 898px; height: 1px; padding-top: 997px; margin-left: 322px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 11px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
• Auth: JWT + Google/GitHub OAuth.
• Feedback: public submissions go through throttling + API-key & origin whitelist (validated via CreatePublicFeedbackDto + global ValidationPipe, content ≤5000 chars); saves to Postgres synchronously, then enqueues a BullMQ job (if plan allows AI).
• Billing: Stripe webhooks at /webhooks/stripe.
• AI: BullMQ worker (ai-analysis queue) calls Gemini, writes the result back to Postgres, then emits Socket.io feedbackUpdated.
• Digest: weekly AI digest — scheduled in-process via @Cron (Mon 09:00). The duplicate EventBridge→Lambda→internal-endpoint trigger was removed 2026-07-03.
• Events Gateway: single event feedbackUpdated (room team-{teamId}) — emitted after AI analysis, on status update, and on bulk archive. NOT emitted on initial feedback creation.
• SQS/Lambda feedback-processor stub removed 2026-07-09 (PLAN 🔥 #9) — it only console.log'd the event and shipped no feature; the seam right after feedbackRepository.save() stays in the code for a future real consumer (Slack, analytics).
• Landing (apps/landing, insightstream.ai): pure marketing Next.js 16 app — zero @insightstream/* deps. All CTAs are plain links to app.insightstream.ai/auth/register. Registration auto-creates a personal team. See Registration & Onboarding Flow tab.

rect.style: stroke: light-dark(rgb(68, 68, 68), rgb(179, 179, 179));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 368px; height: 1px; padding-top: 93px; margin-left: 1292px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 338px; height: 1px; padding-top: 127px; margin-left: 1302px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 11px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
• Monorepo (pnpm + Turbo) — share types/DB entities across apps without publishing packages.
• PostgreSQL over MongoDB — relational data (users/projects/teams/feedback) benefits from FK integrity and TypeORM tooling.
• BullMQ (Redis) for AI analysis — decouples slow Gemini calls from the feedback-submission request/response cycle.
• Socket.io for realtime dashboard updates — pushes AI results and status changes without polling.
• Stateless API — no session state on EC2, safe to scale horizontally or swap ALB target groups.
• AWS migration: API (EC2) and DB (RDS) cutover complete; Web (Vercel→Amplify) still a parallel run — see Current vs Target panel on the AWS Infrastructure tab.

path.style: stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));
